record.field: Memos is a lightweight, self-hosted note-taking service built for fast, frictionless capture of your thoughts in a private timeline.

Memos is a lightweight, self-hosted note-taking service built for fast, frictionless capture of your thoughts in a private timeline.

It centers on a single text box for quick entry and a chronological feed of your notes, all written in portable Markdown and stored on your own infrastructure with zero telemetry. Built as a single Go binary with a React/TypeScript frontend, it deploys in one command and ships full REST and gRPC APIs for integrations. It is a popular open-source alternative to commercial tools like Notion, Evernote and Standard Notes for users who value data ownership and simplicity.

Key features
  • Markdown-native notes with syntax highlighting, tables and LaTeX math
  • Timeline-first interface for quick capture without folders or notebooks
  • Drag-and-drop attachments for images, video, audio and documents
  • Single ~20MB binary/Docker image with SQLite, MySQL or PostgreSQL backends
  • Full REST and gRPC APIs plus keyboard shortcuts for power users
  • Self-hosted with complete data ownership and no telemetry
Best for

Individuals and teams who want a private, portable scratchpad or micro-blog for ideas, daily logs and quick knowledge capture, hosted on their own servers.

services: memos: image: neosmemo/memos:stable restart: unless-stopped volumes: - ./memos_data:/var/opt/memos environment: - MEMOS_MODE={{ memos_mode }} - MEMOS_PORT={{ memos_port }} ports: - '{{ PORT_HTTP }}:5230' container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}' networks: - internal - agz-proxy-network networks: internal: driver: bridge agz-proxy-network: external: true
